# stonebranch_trigger (Data Source)

Looks up a trigger by name from StoneBranch Universal Controller. Returns common fields available across all trigger types.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Stonebranch Trigger Data Source Example
#
# This example demonstrates how to look up an existing trigger by name.
# The data source returns common fields available across all trigger types.

terraform {
  required_providers {
    stonebranch = {
      source = "stonebranch/stonebranch"
    }
  }
}

provider "stonebranch" {
  # Uses STONEBRANCH_API_TOKEN environment variable
}

# Look up an existing trigger by name
data "stonebranch_trigger" "example" {
  name = "my-existing-trigger"
}

# Output the trigger details
output "trigger_type" {
  description = "Type of the trigger (e.g., triggerTime, triggerCron)"
  value       = data.stonebranch_trigger.example.type
}

output "trigger_sys_id" {
  description = "System ID of the trigger"
  value       = data.stonebranch_trigger.example.sys_id
}

output "trigger_enabled" {
  description = "Whether the trigger is enabled"
  value       = data.stonebranch_trigger.example.enabled
}

output "trigger_tasks" {
  description = "Tasks executed by this trigger"
  value       = data.stonebranch_trigger.example.tasks
}

# Example: Use the data source to check trigger status
# locals {
#   trigger_status = data.stonebranch_trigger.example.enabled ? "active" : "disabled"
# }
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) The name of the trigger to look up.

### Read-Only

- `calendar` (String) Calendar used by the trigger.
- `description` (String) Description of the trigger.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the trigger is enabled.
- `opswise_groups` (List of String) List of business service names this trigger belongs to.
- `sys_id` (String) System ID of the trigger.
- `tasks` (List of String) List of task names that this trigger executes.
- `time_zone` (String) Time zone for the trigger.
- `type` (String) Type of the trigger (e.g., 'triggerTime', 'triggerCron', 'triggerFm', 'triggerTm').
- `version` (Number) Version number of the trigger.
